children.text: building the understanding of and confidence in investments in basic research is essential to public support for sustained, predictable Federal funding;1So in original. Probably should be preceded by introductory text.1

the Foundation has improved transparency and accountability of the outcomes made through the merit review process, but additional transparency into individual grants is valuable in communicating and assuring the public value of federally funded research; and

the Foundation should commit to transparency and accountability and to clear, consistent public communication regarding the national interest for each Foundation-awarded grant and cooperative agreement.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director of the Foundation shall issue and periodically update, as appropriate, policy guidance for both Foundation staff and other Foundation merit review process participants on the importance of transparency and accountability to the outcomes made through the merit review process.

children.label: i
children.text: reflects the statutory mission of the Foundation, as established in the National Science Foundation Act of 1950 ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 1861

children.label: ii
children.text: addresses the Foundation’s intellectual merit and broader impacts criteria; and

children.label: B
children.text: clearly identifying the research goals of the project in a manner that can be easily understood by both technical and non-technical audiences.
